answer_explanation: Blending the sounds /s/, /t/, /o/, and /p/ produces the word 'stop,' which contains the consonant blend 'st.' Blending the sounds /s/, /l/, /i/, and /p/ produces the word 'slip,' which contains the consonant blend 'sl.' Option C ('step') is incorrect because it uses the short 'e' vowel sound instead of the short 'o' or short 'i' sounds from the given phoneme groups. Option D ('slap') is incorrect because it uses the short 'a' vowel sound instead of the short 'i' sound from the second phoneme group.
subject: ELA

question: Listen to the word 'ship'. Isolate and pronounce the final sound you hear in 'ship'. Which of the following words have that same final sound? Select ALL that apply.
answer_explanation: The correct answers are A, B, and D. The word 'ship' ends with the /sh/ sound. Option A ('fish') ends with the /sh/ sound. Option B ('dash') ends with the /sh/ sound. Option D ('dish') ends with the /sh/ sound. Option C ('shop') is incorrect because the /sh/ sound is at the beginning of the word, not the end.
subject: ELA

question: Say the word: sit. Which words have the same middle sound as sit? Select ALL that apply.
answer_explanation: The word 'sit' has the short vowel sound /ĭ/ in the middle. Option A (pin) is correct because the middle sound is /ĭ/. Option B (hit) is correct because the middle sound is /ĭ/. Option D (lip) is correct because the middle sound is /ĭ/. Option C (sun) is incorrect because the middle sound is /ŭ/ (short u), which is different from the /ĭ/ sound in 'sit'.
subject: ELA

question: Listen as your teacher says each word below. Select ALL options that correctly show every individual sound (phoneme) you hear in each word.

answer_options.B: stop: s – t – ŏ – p (short o)
answer_options.C: chat: c – h – ă – t (short a)

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the word 'ship' has three individual sounds: the digraph 'sh' makes one sound, followed by the short vowel 'ĭ' and the sound 'p'. Option B is correct because 'stop' has four individual sounds: 's', 't', 'ŏ' (short o), and 'p'; even though 'st' is a blend, each letter represents a separate phoneme. Option D is correct because 'fish' has three individual sounds: 'f', the short vowel 'ĭ', and the digraph 'sh' which makes one sound at the end. Option C is incorrect because it separates the digraph 'ch' into two sounds; 'chat' should be segmented as ch – ă – t (three sounds), not c – h – ă – t.
subject: ELA

question: Say the words 'frog' and 'nest' slowly. Which choices show all the individual sounds you hear? Select ALL that apply.
answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the spoken word 'frog' is made of four individual sounds: /f/ as in fan, /r/ as in run, /ŏ/ (short o) as in hot, and /g/ as in go. Option D is correct because the spoken word 'nest' is made of four individual sounds: /n/ as in nut, /ĕ/ (short e) as in bed, /s/ as in sit, and /t/ as in top. Option B is incorrect because it combines the first two sounds /f/ and /r/ into one blend /fr/ instead of segmenting them into two separate individual sounds. Option C is incorrect because it combines the last two sounds /s/ and /t/ into one blend /st/ instead of segmenting them into two separate individual sounds.
subject: ELA

question: Look at these words. Which words have the /sh/ sound spelled with the letters 's' and 'h' together? Select ALL that apply.
answer_explanation: Option A is correct because 'crush' ends with the consonant digraph 'sh' which makes the /sh/ sound. Option B is correct because 'brush' ends with the consonant digraph 'sh' which makes the /sh/ sound. Option C is correct because 'crash' ends with the consonant digraph 'sh' which makes the /sh/ sound. Option D is incorrect because 'catch' ends with 'tch' which makes the /ch/ sound, not the /sh/ sound.
subject: ELA

question: Read the sentence. 'The fish has a big chin.' Which words have a consonant digraph (two letters that make one sound together)? Select all that apply.
answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the word 'fish' ends with the consonant digraph 'sh,' where the letters 's' and 'h' work together to make the single /sh/ sound. Option C is correct because the word 'chin' starts with the consonant digraph 'ch,' where the letters 'c' and 'h' work together to make the single /ch/ sound. Options B and D are not correct because 'big' and 'has' do not contain any consonant digraphs; each letter in those words represents a single sound.
subject: ELA

question: Which of the following are regularly spelled one-syllable words? Select all that apply.
answer_explanation: Option A (jump) is a regularly spelled one-syllable word following a CVCC pattern with a final consonant blend (/mp/) and a short vowel sound (/ŭ/). Option B (ship) is a regularly spelled one-syllable word with a digraph (/sh/) representing the regular consonant sound, a short vowel (/ĭ/), and a final consonant (/p/). Option D (frog) is a regularly spelled one-syllable word following a CCVC pattern with an initial consonant blend (/fr/), a short vowel (/ŏ/), and a final consonant (/g/). Option C (said) is not regularly spelled because the letters 'ai' do not make their typical long 'a' sound (/ā/) or any other regular predictable sound; instead, they represent the short 'e' sound (/ĕ/), making it an irregular high-frequency word.
subject: ELA

question: Your teacher asks you to read these words aloud. Which words are real and can be sounded out using regular spelling patterns? Select ALL that apply.
answer_explanation: The words 'jump' and 'lamp' are regularly spelled one-syllable words. 'Jump' follows the CVCC pattern with a short vowel sound /ŭ/ and a final consonant blend /mp/ (letter-sounds: /j/, /ŭ/, /m/, /p/). 'Lamp' also follows the CVCC pattern with a short vowel sound /ă/ and the final consonant blend /mp/ (letter-sounds: /l/, /ă/, /m/, /p/). The words 'jamp' and 'lomp' are not real English words and do not follow standard Grade 1 spelling patterns, so they cannot be correctly decoded as valid words.
subject: ELA

question: Read the words below. Which words use a final –e or a vowel team to make a long vowel sound? Select ALL that apply.
answer_explanation: Option A is correct because 'tube' uses the final –e pattern (silent e) to make the long 'u' sound. Option C is correct because 'boat' uses the 'oa' vowel team to make the long 'o' sound. Option D is correct because 'tree' uses the 'ee' vowel team to make the long 'e' sound. Option B is incorrect because 'sad' has a short 'a' sound and does not use a final –e or a vowel team.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the sentence.

"The team will ride to the lake."

Which words have a long vowel sound made by a vowel team or a final -e? Select ALL that apply.
answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the word 'team' contains the vowel team 'ea,' which works together to make the long 'e' sound. Option C is correct because the word 'ride' uses the final -e pattern; the silent 'e' at the end makes the 'i' say its name (long 'i' sound). Option D is correct because the word 'lake' uses the final -e pattern; the silent 'e' at the end makes the 'a' say its name (long 'a' sound).
subject: ELA

question: Sara is clapping out syllables in words. She remembers that every syllable must have a vowel sound. Which words have exactly 2 syllables? Select ALL that apply.
answer_explanation: Option A is correct because 'water' has two syllables: wa-ter. The first syllable has the vowel sound 'a' and the second syllable has the r-controlled vowel sound 'er'. Option C is correct because 'table' has two syllables: ta-ble. The first syllable has the long vowel sound 'a' and the second syllable has the 'le' vowel sound. Option D is correct because 'pencil' has two syllables: pen-cil. The first syllable has the short vowel sound 'e' and the second syllable has the short vowel sound 'i'. Option B is incorrect because 'nest' has only one syllable with a single short vowel sound 'e'.
subject: ELA

question: The word 'water' has two syllables: wa-ter. Each syllable has a vowel sound. Which other words have exactly two syllables? Select ALL that apply.
answer_explanation: Every syllable must have a vowel sound. Option A is correct because 'pencil' has two syllables (pen-cil). The first syllable has the short e sound, and the second syllable has a vowel sound. Option B is correct because 'basket' has two syllables (bas-ket). The first syllable has the short a sound, and the second syllable has the short e sound. Option C is incorrect because 'snake' has only one vowel sound (the long a sound) and therefore only one syllable. Option D is incorrect because 'lamp' has only one vowel sound (the short a sound) and therefore only one syllable.
subject: ELA

question: Look at how these two-syllable words are broken apart. Which words are split into syllables the correct way? Select ALL that apply.
answer_explanation: Option A is correct because 'puppet' follows the VCCV pattern with double consonants 'pp' in the middle, so it is correctly divided between them as pup-pet. Option B is correct because 'button' also follows the VCCV pattern with double consonants 'tt', so it is correctly divided between them as but-ton. Option C is correct because 'robot' follows the VCV pattern, which is correctly divided after the first vowel to create an open first syllable: ro-bot. Option D is incorrect because 'napkin' has two different consonants 'p' and 'k' in the middle (VCCV pattern) and should be divided between them as nap-kin, not na-pkin.
subject: ELA

question: Look at how each word is broken into parts. Which words are split into syllables the correct way? Select ALL that apply.
answer_explanation: Option A is correct because 'basket' follows the VCCV pattern with two consonants (s and k) between the vowels; we split between them to get 'bas/ket.' Option B is correct because 'kitten' follows the VCCV pattern with a doubled consonant (tt); we split between the two t's to get 'kit/ten.' Option D is correct because 'bedroom' is a compound word made of two smaller words, 'bed' and 'room,' so we split between the words to get 'bed/room.' Option C is incorrect because 'rocket' contains the consonant digraph 'ck,' which makes one sound; we never split digraphs, so the correct split is 'rock/et,' not 'ro/cket.'
subject: ELA

question:
Read these words from the sentence: The kitties chased the flies near the boxes.

Which words have the inflectional ending that means 'more than one'? Select ALL that apply.
answer_explanation: Option A is correct because 'kitties' ends in '-ies,' which is the inflectional ending that shows more than one kitten (when a word ends in 'y,' the 'y' changes to 'i' before adding 'es'). Option C is correct because 'flies' uses the '-ies' ending to show more than one fly. Option D is correct because 'boxes' ends in '-es,' which is the inflectional ending added to words ending in 'x' to show more than one box. Option B is incorrect because 'chased' ends in '-ed,' which is an inflectional ending that shows the action happened in the past, not that there is more than one.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the sentence.

The dogs barked and are running.

Which words have an inflectional ending (like -s, -ed, or -ing)? Select ALL that apply.
answer_explanation: The words 'dogs,' 'barked,' and 'running' all contain inflectional endings. 'Dogs' is formed from the base word 'dog' plus the ending '-s' to show more than one. 'Barked' is formed from the base word 'bark' plus the ending '-ed' to show the action happened in the past. 'Running' is formed from the base word 'run' plus the ending '-ing' to show the action is happening now. The word 'and' is a connecting word that does not have an inflectional ending added to it.
subject: ELA

question: Which of the following words are irregularly spelled? Select ALL that apply.
answer_explanation: The word 'was' (Option A) is irregularly spelled because the letter 'a' represents the /ʌ/ sound (as in 'cup') rather than the typical short 'a' /æ/ sound, and the final 's' is pronounced /z/, not /s/. The word 'the' (Option C) is irregularly spelled because the 'th' represents the voiced /ð/ sound and the 'e' represents the schwa /ə/ sound, not the standard sounds typically taught for those letters in isolation. The word 'said' (Option D) is irregularly spelled because the letters 'ai' represent the /ɛ/ sound (short e) instead of the long /eɪ/ sound that 'ai' usually represents in words like 'rain' or 'train'. The word 'cat' (Option B) is not irregularly spelled because it follows the standard consonant-vowel-consonant (CVC) pattern where each letter makes its typical sound: 'c' says /k/, 'a' says /æ/ (short a), and 't' says /t/.
subject: ELA

question: Which of the following demonstrate the skill described in RF.1.3.G at medium difficulty? (Select ALL that apply)

answer_options.A: A correct choice that matches the standard.
answer_options.B: Another correct choice that also matches.

answer_explanation: Options A and B are correct because they match the skill described in the standard. Options C and D are incorrect.
subject: ELA

question: Read the story: "Pam sees dark clouds in the sky. She runs inside to get her coat. Soon it begins to rain. Pam stays dry under the porch." Which sentences are true based on the story? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Pam goes inside because she sees clouds.
answer_options.B: It rains after Pam gets her coat.
answer_options.C: Pam gets her hat from inside.
answer_options.D: Pam stays dry during the rain.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the story states Pam sees dark clouds in the sky and then runs inside, showing she goes inside because of the clouds. Option B is correct because the story says she gets her coat inside, and then soon it begins to rain, showing the rain happens after she gets her coat. Option D is correct because the story states Pam stays dry under the porch while it is raining. Option C is incorrect because the story specifically says Pam gets her coat from inside, not a hat.
subject: ELA

question: Read the sentences. Pam sees a bird in a tree. The bird is yellow. It sings a song. Which sentences tell what the text says? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.C: The bird is in a tree.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the first sentence of the text states that Pam sees a bird. Option B is correct because the second sentence states the bird is yellow. Option C is correct because the first sentence states the bird is in a tree. Option D is incorrect because the text states the bird is yellow, not red.
subject: ELA

question: Eli is reading a short story aloud for the second time. The first time he read it, he said “sip” for the word “ship” and read very slowly, sounding out each letter. Which actions show that Eli is reading with fluency this time? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: He says “ship” correctly without skipping the word.
answer_options.B: He reads very fast, skipping any words he remembers were hard before.
answer_options.C: He makes his voice go up at the end of questions and down at the end of statements.
answer_options.D: He reads at a speed that sounds like natural talking, not stopping at every single letter.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because reading with accuracy means saying each word correctly (fixing “sip” to “ship”). Option C is correct because reading with expression means changing your voice to match punctuation marks. Option D is correct because reading at an appropriate rate means moving smoothly at a talking pace instead of sounding out every letter slowly. Option B is incorrect because skipping words reduces accuracy, which is required for fluent reading even on successive readings.
subject: ELA

question: Mia is reading a story aloud to her teacher. This is the second time she has read the story today. Which choices show that she is reading fluently (with accuracy, appropriate rate, or expression)? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: She reads each word correctly and does not skip any lines.
answer_options.B: She reads as fast as she can to finish the page quickly.
answer_options.C: She makes her voice go up when she sees a question mark.
answer_options.D: She stops to fix a word she read wrong and then reads it correctly.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because reading each word correctly without skipping lines demonstrates accuracy, which is a key part of fluency. Option C is correct because changing her voice for punctuation shows expression and understanding of the text. Option D is correct because self-correcting a mistake shows that she is monitoring for accuracy. Option B is incorrect because reading as fast as possible without caring about mistakes is not an appropriate rate; fluency means reading at a pace that supports understanding, not just speed.
subject: ELA

question: Read the story. 'The sun is h_t. We need shade. We are sweating. We want to cool off.' At first, Liz read the word in the blank as 'hat,' but that did not make sense. Which sentences help Liz use context to confirm the correct word? Select ALL that apply.
answer_explanation: Option A is correct because needing shade is something you do when it is hot, which helps confirm the word is 'hot' and not 'hat.' Option B is correct because sweating happens when you are hot, providing a clue that the word describes heat. Option D is correct because wanting to cool off is a reaction to hot weather, helping confirm the meaning. Option C is incorrect because it is the sentence containing the unknown word itself; it does not provide context clues to help figure out the word, but rather is the word being figured out.
subject: ELA

question: Read the sentence: 'It is hot outside. I want to drink some _____.' Which words make sense in the blank? Select all that apply.
answer_explanation: Option A (water) is correct because water is a cold drink that helps you cool down when it is hot outside. Option B (juice) is correct because juice is also a cold drink that makes sense to have in hot weather. Option C (lemonade) is correct because lemonade is a cold drink people enjoy on hot days. Option D (hot cocoa) is incorrect because it is a hot drink meant for cold weather, so it does not fit the context of a hot day.
subject: ELA

question: Read the text: 'Honeybees live in a hive with many other bees. They fly from flower to flower to find nectar. Bees make honey inside the hive. They work together to keep the hive safe.' Which sentences from the text tell where bees live or what bees make? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Honeybees live in a hive with many other bees.
answer_options.B: They fly from flower to flower to find nectar.
answer_options.C: Bees make honey inside the hive.
answer_options.D: They work together to keep the hive safe.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because it tells where bees live (in a hive). Option C is correct because it tells what bees make (honey). Option B is incorrect because it describes how bees find food (flying to flowers), not where they live or what they make. Option D is incorrect because it describes how bees behave to protect their home, not where they live or what they make.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the sentences about squirrels.

Squirrels gather nuts in fall. They store food for winter. Squirrels climb trees every day.

Which sentences from the text tell when squirrels do something? Select all that apply.
answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the text states that squirrels gather nuts in fall, which tells when they gather nuts. Option B is correct because the text states that squirrels store food for winter, which tells when they store food. Option C is correct because the text states that squirrels climb trees every day, which tells when they climb. Option D is incorrect because the text does not say that squirrels eat nuts at night.
subject: ELA

question: Your teacher is helping you choose an informational book to read. You open a book about sea turtles. Which things tell you this book is just right for a first grader to read with a teacher's help? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: It has photographs that show the sea turtles and their eggs.
answer_options.B: The sentences are short, and some words are in a glossary at the back.
answer_options.C: It has a list of chapters at the beginning called a table of contents.
answer_options.D: It has long paragraphs with tiny print and no pictures to help.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because photographs provide visual support that helps first graders understand the information. Option B is correct because short sentences and a glossary make the text appropriately complex with support for new vocabulary. Option C is correct because a table of contents is a text feature that helps first graders find information with teacher support. Option D is incorrect because long paragraphs, tiny print, and no pictures would make the book too difficult for a first grader even with help.
subject: ELA

question: Your teacher asks you to pick an informational book to read together during class. Which books are good for a first grader to read with help? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: A book about farms with photos and one sentence under each picture.
answer_options.B: A book about planets with a table of contents and short facts.
answer_options.C: A book with tiny print and long paragraphs about ancient history.
answer_options.D: A book about seasons with big words but picture clues on every page.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because photos and short sentences are features of informational texts written at a Grade 1 level. Option B is correct because a table of contents and short facts are appropriate text features for first graders reading with teacher support. Option C is incorrect because tiny print and long paragraphs make a text too complex for Grade 1 readers. Option D is correct because picture clues provide the prompting and support needed to figure out challenging words, making the text appropriately complex for Grade 1 with help.
subject: ELA

question: Read the paragraph. 'Bees live together in a hive. They fly from flower to flower to collect nectar. Worker bees turn the nectar into sweet honey. They store the honey inside the hive to eat later when flowers are gone.' First, think about what the text is mostly about. Which sentences tell key details about that main topic? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.B: Worker bees turn nectar into honey.
answer_options.C: Bees store honey to eat later.
answer_options.D: Bees make their homes in trees.

answer_explanation: Options A, B, and C are correct because they are key details from the text that explain how bees make honey. The text states that bees fly to flowers to collect nectar, worker bees turn that nectar into honey, and they store the honey to eat later when flowers are gone. Option D is incorrect because the text says bees live in a hive, not in trees.
subject: ELA

question: Read the sentences. 'Bears are big animals. They sleep in winter. Bears like to eat fish.' Which sentences tell about bears in the text? Select ALL that apply.
answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the text states that 'Bears are big animals.' Option B is correct because the text states that 'They sleep in winter.' Option C is incorrect because the text says bears like to eat fish, not honey. Option D is incorrect because the text does not say bears can fly, and this is not true about bears.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the text about bees and flowers.

Bees visit flowers to find sweet nectar. They use the nectar to make honey inside their hive. When bees move from flower to flower, they carry pollen on their legs. This helps the flowers grow seeds for new plants.

How are bees and flowers connected? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Bees use flowers to make honey.
answer_options.D: Bees eat pollen to make honey.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the text states that bees find nectar in flowers and then use that nectar to make honey. Option B is correct because the text explains that when bees carry pollen between flowers, it helps the flowers grow seeds. Option C is incorrect because the text says bees make the honey using nectar, not the flowers themselves. Option D is incorrect because the text states bees collect nectar to make honey, not pollen; the pollen is carried to help flowers, not eaten by bees.
subject: ELA

question: Read the text. "Bees visit flowers to find nectar. They use the nectar to make honey. When bees visit flowers, they move pollen around. This helps the flowers make seeds." What connections does the text describe? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Bees get nectar from the flowers.
answer_options.B: Bees use nectar to make honey.
answer_options.C: Flowers make honey for the bees.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the text states that bees visit flowers to find nectar, showing a connection between bees and flowers. Option B is correct because the text describes how bees use the nectar (from flowers) to make honey, connecting these two pieces of information. Option D is correct because the text explains that when bees visit flowers, they move pollen around, which helps the flowers make seeds, showing another connection between bees and flowers. Option C is incorrect because the text states that bees make the honey, not the flowers.
subject: ELA

question: Read the sentences about an owl. 'The owl is nocturnal. It opens its big eyes when the stars are out. It hunts for food while you are sleeping in your bed. The owl has soft brown feathers.' Which details from the text help you understand what the word 'nocturnal' means? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.B: while you are sleeping in your bed

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the stars appearing in the sky is a clue that it is nighttime, helping the reader infer that 'nocturnal' means active at night. Option B is correct because sleeping in bed happens during the night, which provides context that the owl is awake while it is dark outside. Option C is incorrect because opening big eyes describes an action the owl takes, but it does not indicate the time of day or help explain the meaning of 'nocturnal.' Option D is incorrect because having soft brown feathers describes the owl's physical appearance, which is not related to when the owl is awake or active.
subject: ELA

question: Read the sentences. "When it gets cold in winter, many birds fly far away to warmer places. They migrate to find food and stay warm. The journey is long and tiring." Which words or phrases from the text help you understand what the word migrate means? Select ALL that apply.
answer_explanation: A is correct because flying far away describes the action of moving from one place to another, which is what migrate means. B is correct because finding food explains the reason the birds move, helping to clarify the purpose of migrating. C is correct because staying warm explains another reason the birds travel to a new place, which helps define what migrate means in this context. D is incorrect because long and tiring describes how the journey feels rather than explaining what the word migrate means.
subject: ELA

question:
Look at the parts of a book about bears below.

**Table of Contents**
Chapter 1: All About Bears … page 3
Chapter 2: What Bears Eat … page 8

**Glossary**
cub: a baby bear
den: a home where a bear sleeps

Tara is reading this sentence in the book: "The mother bear takes her ______ to the ______ to sleep." She wants to find the meaning of the words "cub" and "den" to fill in the blanks. Which text features should Tara use? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.D: The table of contents page numbers

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the glossary entry for "cub" defines the word as "a baby bear," which tells Tara what word fills the first blank. Option B is correct because the glossary entry for "den" defines it as "a home where a bear sleeps," which matches the second blank about where they sleep. Option C is incorrect because the chapter "What Bears Eat" would contain facts about bear food, not definitions of the words "cub" or "den." Option D is incorrect because while the table of contents helps readers find chapters by page number, it does not provide definitions for words.
subject: ELA

question: Read this sentence from a book about ocean animals: 'A shark is a predator that hunts for fish.' Mia wants to find the page number where the chapter 'Ocean Hunters' begins. She also wants to know what the word 'predator' means. Which text features should Mia use to find this information? Select ALL that apply.
answer_explanation: Option A is correct because a table of contents lists the chapters in a book and the page numbers where they begin, which helps Mia find the page number for the 'Ocean Hunters' chapter. Option B is correct because a glossary lists important words from the book and their definitions, which helps Mia learn what the word 'predator' means. Option C is incorrect because a title page only shows the book's title and author, not chapter page numbers or word definitions. Option D is incorrect because a heading shows the name of a section within the text but does not list page numbers before the chapter starts or provide definitions for words.
subject: ELA

question: Read the words: 'Penguins live in cold places. They slide on their bellies to move fast.'. Snowflakes are falling from the sky. Which details do you learn ONLY from the picture and NOT from the words? Select ALL that apply?
answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the picture shows the penguins are black and white, but the words do not describe their color. Option D is correct because the picture shows snow falling, but the words do not mention snow or weather. Option B is incorrect because the words explicitly state that penguins live in cold places, even though the picture shows snow. Option C is incorrect because both the words and the picture tell us that penguins slide on their bellies; it is not information learned ONLY from the picture.
subject: ELA

question: Read the sentence: 'The rabbit likes the carrot.'. Which details does the picture show that the sentence does NOT tell you? Select ALL that apply?
answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the sentence does not tell the color of the rabbit, but the picture shows it is white. Option B is correct because the sentence does not describe the rabbit's ears, but the picture shows they are long. Option D is correct because the sentence does not tell the color of the carrot, but the picture shows it is orange. Option C is incorrect because the sentence explicitly states that 'The rabbit likes the carrot,' so this information comes from the words, not just the picture.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the text and look at the illustration described below.

Text: Seeds need water and warmth to grow into plants. A small root pushes down into the soil.

Illustration: The picture shows a seed in dark soil, a small green sprout with tiny leaves reaching up toward the sky, and raindrops falling from gray clouds above.

Which statements use details from BOTH the text and the illustration to describe the key idea of how seeds grow? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Seeds begin to grow when they get water from the rain and warmth.
answer_options.B: The root of the new plant grows down into the soil.
answer_options.C: The fully grown plant has large red flowers.
answer_options.D: Seeds stay buried in the soil and never change.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because it combines the text detail that seeds need 'water and warmth' with the illustration detail of 'raindrops falling' to describe how seeds start to grow. Option B is correct because it uses the text detail that 'a small root pushes down into the soil' together with the illustration detail showing a 'seed in dark soil' to describe how the plant develops. Option C is incorrect because the illustration only shows a small sprout with tiny leaves, not large flowers. Option D is incorrect because both the text and the illustration show the seed changing and growing into a plant, not staying the same forever.
subject: ELA

question: Frogs live near ponds. This frog sits on a big green leaf. It has long back legs for jumping. Which details tell about the frog? Select ALL that apply?

answer_options.A: It sits on a big green leaf.
answer_options.D: It lives in a sandy desert.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the text states the frog sits on a big green leaf and the picture shows the frog on a leaf. Option B is correct because the text mentions long back legs and the picture shows this detail. Option C is incorrect because neither the text nor the picture shows a frog with a long tail. Option D is incorrect because the text says frogs live near ponds, not in sandy deserts, and the picture shows water, not sand.
subject: ELA

question: Read the sentences: "Frogs must live near water to stay alive. Their skin needs to stay wet so they can breathe. Water keeps their eggs safe from drying out." First, find the main point the author is making. Then, find the reasons the author gives to support that point. Which sentences tell WHY frogs must live near water? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.C: Water keeps eggs from drying out.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because it states the reason that frogs need water—wet skin helps them breathe. Option C is correct because it gives another reason—water keeps their eggs safe from drying out. Option B is incorrect because it states the main point itself, not a reason that supports the point. Option D is incorrect because the text does not mention jumping ability as a reason frogs need water.
subject: ELA

question: Read the sentences. "Frogs are interesting animals. They can jump very high to catch bugs. They also make loud sounds called croaks." Which reasons does the author give to show that frogs are interesting? Select all that apply.

answer_options.A: They can jump very high to catch bugs.
answer_options.B: They make loud sounds called croaks.
answer_options.C: They live in ponds and lakes.

answer_explanation: The author states that frogs are interesting and gives specific reasons in the text. Option A is correct because the text says, "They can jump very high to catch bugs." Option B is correct because the text says, "They also make loud sounds called croaks." Option C is incorrect because the author does not mention where frogs live as a reason they are interesting. Option D is incorrect because the author does not describe the color of the frog's skin in the text.
subject: ELA

question:
Text A: Dogs are pets. They have fur on their bodies. Dogs like to play fetch. They need water to drink.

Text B: Dogs are pets. They have four legs. Dogs like to play fetch. They need water to drink.

Read the two texts about dogs. Which facts are told in BOTH texts? Select ALL that apply.
answer_explanation: Option A is correct because both Text A and Text B state that dogs are pets. Option B is correct because both texts mention that dogs like to play fetch. Option C is correct because both texts say dogs need water to drink. Option D is incorrect because only Text B mentions that dogs have four legs; Text A mentions fur instead, so this is not a shared fact.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the two texts about frogs. Then select ALL the sentences that tell about both texts.

Text 1: Frogs live near water. Frogs jump far. Frogs lay eggs in the water.
Text 2: Frogs live near water. Frogs jump far. Frogs breathe with lungs.

Which sentences tell something that is the same in both texts? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.C: Frogs lay eggs in the water.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because both Text 1 and Text 2 state that frogs live near water. Option B is correct because both texts mention that frogs jump far. Option C is incorrect because only Text 1 mentions that frogs lay eggs in the water; Text 2 does not mention this. Option D is incorrect because only Text 2 mentions that frogs breathe with lungs; Text 1 does not mention this.
subject: ELA

question: Read the story: Maya was excited for her birthday. She smiled all morning. She jumped up and down. She opened her presents quickly. Which details show that Maya was excited? Select all that apply.
answer_explanation: Option A is correct because smiling all morning shows Maya was happy and excited. Option B is correct because jumping up and down is a way people show excitement. Option C is correct because opening presents quickly shows eager, excited behavior. Option D is incorrect because it only tells when the story happens, not how Maya showed her excitement.
subject: ELA

question: Read the story. Maya found a yellow kitten under the porch. She gave it a bowl of milk. The kitten licked its paws and purred. Which sentences tell key details from the story? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.B: The kitten was under the porch.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the story states that Maya found a yellow kitten. Option B is correct because the story explicitly says the kitten was under the porch. Option C is correct because the story tells us that Maya gave it a bowl of milk. Option D is incorrect because the story describes the kitten as yellow, not black.
subject: ELA

question:
Read this short poem:

The rain falls down.
It splashes on the ground.
The flowers drink it up.
They grow so big and round.

Which features tell you this poem is just right for a Grade 1 reader? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: It uses short words that rhyme, like 'down' and 'ground'.
answer_options.B: It tells about things Grade 1 students know, like rain and flowers.
answer_options.C: It has long words with many letters that are hard to sound out.
answer_options.D: It has a rhythm that makes it easy to read aloud.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because Grade 1 texts use simple, rhyming words that help students predict and decode. Option B is correct because familiar topics make the text easier to understand. Option C is incorrect because long, difficult words would make the text too hard for Grade 1 readers. Option D is correct because rhythm and predictable patterns support fluency, which is appropriate for Grade 1.
subject: ELA

question: Your teacher asks you to pick books that are just right for Grade 1. Which books should you pick? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: A story with short sentences and pictures about a puppy.
answer_options.B: A long chapter book with no pictures and hard words.
answer_options.C: A short poem about the sun that rhymes.
answer_options.D: A thick science book with tiny letters and big words.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because stories with short sentences, picture support, and familiar topics like animals match the appropriate complexity for Grade 1 prose. Option C is correct because brief poems with simple rhyme patterns and concrete, familiar subjects like the sun represent grade-appropriate poetry. Option B is incorrect because lengthy chapter books lacking illustrations and containing advanced vocabulary exceed Grade 1 reading complexity. Option D is incorrect because dense informational texts with small print and difficult scientific terminology are too complex for Grade 1 readers.
subject: ELA

question: Read the story. Max finds a bird with a hurt wing. He takes the bird to a vet. The vet helps the bird fly away. Which choices tell key details from the story OR show the lesson? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Max finds a bird with a hurt wing.
answer_options.B: Max takes the bird to the vet.
answer_options.C: Max keeps the bird as a pet.
answer_options.D: It is kind to help animals.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because it tells the key detail that Max finds a bird with a hurt wing. Option B is correct because it tells the key detail that Max takes the bird to the vet to get help. Option D is correct because it states the central lesson of the story: helping animals is a kind thing to do. Option C is incorrect because the story says Max takes the bird to the vet, not that he keeps it as a pet.
subject: ELA

question: Lucy sees a cat stuck in a tree. She asks her mom for help. They get a ladder and save the cat. Lucy feels proud. Which sentences tell key details from the story OR show the lesson? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Lucy sees a cat stuck in a tree.
answer_options.B: Lucy and her mom save the cat.
answer_options.D: It is kind to help animals in need.

answer_explanation: Option A tells a key detail from the story about what Lucy sees at the beginning. Option B tells a key detail about how Lucy and her mom solved the problem. Option D states the central message or lesson of the story about being kind to animals.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the story.

Early one morning, Sam the cat sat by the window. He watched birds in the tall green tree. When Sam tried to jump up, his paw got stuck on the curtain. Mom came to help him down.

Which sentences use key details to describe the characters, setting, or major events in the story? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: The story takes place early in the morning.
answer_options.B: Sam is a cat who watches birds by the window.
answer_options.C: Mom helps Sam get down from the curtain.
answer_options.D: Mom watches birds in the tall tree.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because it uses the key detail 'early one morning' to describe the setting. Option B is correct because it uses key details 'cat' and 'watched birds by the window' to describe the character Sam. Option C is correct because it uses the key detail 'Mom came to help him down' to describe a major event at the end of the story. Option D is incorrect because the text states that Sam watched the birds, not Mom.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the story.

Tom is a happy boy with brown hair. He plays in his small backyard. Tom finds a red ball under the green bush.

Which sentences use key details to describe the characters, setting, or events in the story? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.B: Tom plays in his small backyard.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because it uses the key detail 'brown hair' from the story to describe the character Tom. Option B is correct because it uses the key details 'small' and 'backyard' to describe the setting and what Tom does there. Option C is incorrect because the story states that the ball is red, not blue. Option D is incorrect because the story states that the bush is green, not purple.
subject: ELA

question: ... Select ALL that apply.
subject: ELA

question: Read the sentences. The bright sun was hot on my skin. I felt happy to be at the park. Which words tell about a feeling or appeal to the senses? Select all that apply.
answer_explanation: The word 'bright' appeals to the sense of sight by describing how the sun looks. The word 'hot' appeals to the sense of touch by describing how the sun feels on the skin. The word 'happy' suggests a feeling or emotion. The word 'sun' is incorrect because it simply names an object and does not describe a feeling or a sensory experience.
subject: ELA

question: Jake is comparing two books about elephants. The first book is titled 'Amazing Elephants.' It has photographs of real elephants, headings like 'What Elephants Eat,' and teaches true facts about elephant families. The second book is titled 'Ella the Elephant.' It has drawings of an elephant who wears a pink hat, loses it at school, and gets help from a bird to find it. Which sentences show a major difference between a book that gives information and a book that tells a story? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: 'Amazing Elephants' has photographs of real elephants.
answer_options.B: 'Ella the Elephant' has an elephant who wears clothes and loses something.
answer_options.C: 'Amazing Elephants' has headings that tell what each part teaches.
answer_options.D: Both books teach facts about what elephants like to eat.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because informational books use real photographs to show true things, while story books use drawings. Option B is correct because story books have made-up characters that do things real animals cannot do, like wearing clothes and going to school, which shows the book is fiction. Option C is correct because informational books use headings to organize facts for the reader, a feature not found in story books. Option D is incorrect because it describes a similarity rather than a difference; while the informational book teaches facts about eating, the story book focuses on entertaining the reader with a tale about losing a hat, not on teaching dinner facts.
subject: ELA

question: Maya is looking at a book about sharks. She wants to know if it is a book that tells a story or a book that gives information. Which clues show that this is a book that gives information? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: It has photographs of real sharks swimming in the ocean.
answer_options.B: It teaches facts about what sharks eat.
answer_options.C: The shark wears a hat and talks to fish friends.
answer_options.D: It has headings that tell what each page teaches.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because books that give information use real photographs to show true things about animals. Option B is correct because teaching facts about an animal's diet is something information books do. Option D is correct because headings that label the topic of each page are features of information books. Option C is incorrect because a talking animal that wears clothes is a made-up character found in story books, not in books that give true information.
subject: ELA

question:
Read these three sentences from a story.

Sentence 1: The dog barked at the mail carrier.
Sentence 2: "I am protecting my house," said the dog.
Sentence 3: Then the dog wagged its tail.

Which sentences are told by the narrator? Select ALL that apply.
answer_explanation: Sentence 1 and Sentence 3 are told by the narrator because they describe the dog using third-person words like 'the dog' and 'its,' showing that someone outside the story is telling what happened. Sentence 2 uses the word 'I' inside quotation marks, which means the dog is speaking for itself, so the dog (not the narrator) is telling that part of the story. Therefore, option D is also incorrect because it claims the narrator tells all three sentences.
subject: ELA

question: Read these sentences. Which sentences show that a character is telling the story? Select ALL that apply.
answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the word 'I' indicates that a character is telling the story from their own perspective. Option C is correct because the word 'My' shows that a character is describing something that belongs to them, which means the character is the narrator. Option B is incorrect because it uses the words 'The girl,' which indicates an outside narrator is telling the story about someone else. Option D is incorrect because it uses the word 'He,' which indicates an outside narrator is telling the story about another character rather than the character speaking for themselves.
subject: ELA

question: Read the story. "Maya was ready for the big race. She tied her red shoes tight. Maya saw tall trees behind her. The sky was cloudy and gray. The starting gun made a loud bang. Maya ran fast down the path." Which details from the story describe the SETTING? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.C: The loud bang of the gun

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the tall trees describe the place where the story happens, which is part of the setting. Option D is correct because the cloudy gray sky describes the weather and environment, which is part of the setting. Option B is incorrect because Maya's red shoes describe what the character is wearing, not where the story takes place. Option C is incorrect because the loud bang of the gun describes something that happens in the story (an event), not the location where the story happens.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the story and look at the illustration details below.

Story: Kim plays at the park on a windy day.

Illustration details: The picture shows Kim holding a blue kite. She stands on a brown dirt path. There is a tall slide behind her. Leaves are blowing in the air around her.

Which details from the illustration describe the characters or setting? Select ALL that apply.
answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the blue kite is a detail shown in the illustration that describes what the character Kim is holding. Option B is correct because the tall slide is a detail in the illustration that describes part of the park setting. Option D is correct because the blowing leaves are a detail in the illustration that shows the windy weather in the setting. Option C is incorrect because the illustration describes a brown dirt path, not green grass.
subject: ELA

question: Read the story. 'Ben went to the beach and built a sandcastle. He ate ice cream when he was done. Ava stayed home and built a snowman. She drank hot cocoa when she was done. Both children used their hands to shape their creations.' Which sentences tell how Ben and Ava's experiences were the same? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.C: They both had a treat after they finished.
answer_options.D: They both used their hands to make something.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the story states that Ben built a sandcastle and Ava built a snowman, showing that both characters built something. Option C is correct because Ben ate ice cream and Ava drank hot cocoa after finishing their creations, showing both enjoyed a treat. Option D is correct because the story explicitly states that both children used their hands to shape their creations.
subject: ELA

question:
Story 1: Ana hikes up a hill. She finds a blue flower. She feels tired.

Story 2: Ben hikes up a hill. He finds a red rock. He feels tired.

Which things happened to BOTH Ana and Ben? Select ALL that apply.
answer_explanation: Option A is correct because both Ana and Ben hike up a hill. Option C is correct because both Ana and Ben feel tired. Option B is incorrect because only Ana finds a blue flower. Option D is incorrect because only Ben finds a red rock.
subject: ELA

question: During a class discussion about the story 'The Dragon and the Moon,' the teacher asks students to share their ideas. Which actions show that students are following the agreed-upon rules for discussions? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Keisha waits for a pause in the conversation before she shares her idea.
answer_options.B: Marcus looks at the chart paper while his classmate reads from it and points to the words he hears.
answer_options.C: Angela raises her hand and tells a story about her pet dog when the class is discussing dragons.
answer_options.D: David looks at the speaker and keeps his hands quietly in his lap while listening.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because waiting for a natural pause before speaking demonstrates the rule of speaking one at a time without interrupting others. Option B is correct because tracking the words on the chart paper while a classmate reads shows active listening with care and staying focused on the text under discussion. Option D is correct because making eye contact with the speaker and keeping hands still shows respectful, attentive listening to others with care. Option C is incorrect because sharing a story about a pet dog when the discussion topic is a dragon story violates the rule of speaking about the topics and texts under discussion, even though raising a hand is polite.
subject: ELA

question: The class is having a discussion about a story they read. Which students are following the agreed-upon rules for discussions? Select all that apply.

answer_options.A: Jade raises her hand and waits for the teacher to call on her before she speaks.
answer_options.B: Marcus looks at the speaker and nods his head to show he is listening with care.
answer_options.C: Sofia shouts out her idea while another student is still talking.
answer_options.D: Tyler looks down at his desk and draws a picture while his classmate shares.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because waiting to be called on before speaking follows the rule of speaking one at a time. Option B is correct because looking at the speaker and nodding demonstrates listening with care. Option C is incorrect because shouting out while another student is talking breaks the rule of speaking one at a time. Option D is incorrect because looking away and drawing instead of paying attention violates the rule of listening with care.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the conversation between Lucy and Ben.

Lucy: "I found a cocoon in my backyard. It is hanging from a branch."
Ben: "What color is it?"
Lucy: "It is brown and looks like a tiny sleeping bag."

Which questions could Ben ask next to build on what Lucy just said? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Does it feel soft like a pillow?
answer_options.B: Will it turn into a butterfly soon?
answer_options.C: Do you have any toys in your room?
answer_options.D: What did you eat for breakfast?

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because it asks about the texture of the cocoon, building on Lucy's description of it looking like a tiny sleeping bag. Option B is correct because it asks about what will happen to the cocoon, staying on the topic of Lucy's discovery and keeping the conversation going.
subject: ELA

question: Ben says, "I got a new puppy for my birthday. He is very fluffy." Which sentences build on what Ben said to keep the conversation going? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.C: Does he like to play fetch?

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because asking the puppy's name responds directly to Ben's comment about his new puppy. Option C is correct because asking if the puppy plays fetch builds on the topic of the puppy and invites more conversation. Option B is incorrect because asking about the weather does not respond to Ben's comment about his puppy. Option D is incorrect because asking for the time does not build on Ben's talk about his birthday or his new pet.
subject: ELA

question: We read a story about a boy who made a snack. He mixed things together and put it in a cold place. Later, he shared it with his friend. Which questions would help clear up confusion about the story? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: What snack did the boy make?
answer_options.B: Which cold place did he use?
answer_options.C: Did he share it with a friend?

answer_explanation: Options A, B, and D are correct because the story mentions that the boy made a snack, used a cold place, and mixed things together, but it does not specify what the snack was, which exact cold place he used, or what ingredients he mixed. Asking these questions would clear up confusion about missing details. Option C is incorrect because the story already clearly states that he shared the snack with his friend, so asking this question would not clear up any confusion about the text.
subject: ELA

question: Ms. Park read a story about a penguin that walked across the ice to find its family. Some students were confused about what happened in the story. Which questions could students ask to clear up their confusion about the text? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Why did the penguin need to find its family?
answer_options.B: How did the penguin stay warm on the ice?
answer_options.C: Have you ever seen a penguin at the zoo?
answer_options.D: Can penguins fly like other birds?

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because asking why the penguin needed to find its family clarifies the motivation and main problem in the story. Option B is correct because asking how the penguin stayed warm clarifies an important detail about how the penguin survived in the story. Option C is incorrect because it asks about a student's personal experience rather than clearing up confusion about the story's content. Option D is incorrect because it asks a general science question about penguins instead of asking about what happened in the specific story.
subject: ELA

question: Ms. Johnson told her class: 'Yesterday I visited the City Museum. I entered through the glass doors at nine o'clock. First, I looked at the dinosaur bones in the tall room with the blue ceiling. Then, I ate my sandwich in the garden next to the fountain. Before I left, I bought a purple pencil at the gift shop.' Which questions could you answer using the details Ms. Johnson shared? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: What time did Ms. Johnson enter the museum?
answer_options.B: What color was the ceiling in the dinosaur room?
answer_options.C: What did Ms. Johnson eat in the garden?
answer_options.D: What did Ms. Johnson look at after she ate lunch?

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because Ms. Johnson stated she entered at nine o'clock. Option B is correct because she described the ceiling in the dinosaur room as blue. Option C is correct because she mentioned eating her sandwich in the garden. Option D is incorrect because Ms. Johnson looked at the dinosaur bones before eating lunch, not after; this option describes a sequence that did not happen.
subject: ELA

question: Listen to the story the teacher reads aloud: 'The class went on a field trip to the apple orchard. They saw rows of green and red apple trees. Each student picked five apples and put them in a brown paper bag. On the bus ride home, they sang songs about fall.' Which details tell what happened in the story? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: The class visited an apple orchard.
answer_options.B: The students picked five apples each.
answer_options.D: The students ate apples on the bus.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the story states that the class went on a field trip to the apple orchard. Option B is correct because the story says each student picked five apples and put them in a bag. Option C is incorrect because the story mentions the trees had both green and red apples, not that all the apples were only green. Option D is incorrect because the students sang songs on the bus ride home; the story does not say they ate the apples on the bus.
subject: ELA

question: Jake says: 'I need to bring a round object for our class activity tomorrow, but I can't find it.' Which questions would help you understand what Jake needs or clarify what he means? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: What round object are you looking for?
answer_options.B: What class activity are you doing?
answer_options.C: Is your object bumpy or smooth?
answer_options.D: I have a round ball at home.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because asking what round object Jake is looking for helps clarify exactly which item he has lost. Option B is correct because asking about the class activity gathers additional information that could help figure out what object is needed. Option C is incorrect because asking about texture does not help identify which round object Jake needs to find. Option D is incorrect because it is a statement, not a question, so it does not help clarify or gather information about Jake's problem.
subject: ELA

question: Your friend says, "I got a new pet yesterday." Which questions would help you learn more about what your friend said? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: What kind of pet did you get?
answer_options.D: Where did you get the pet?

answer_explanation: A is correct because asking what kind of pet gathers specific information about the new pet the friend mentioned. B is correct because asking for the pet's name clarifies a detail about the new pet. D is correct because asking where the pet was obtained gathers additional information about the pet mentioned. C is incorrect because asking about school does not clarify or add information about the friend's new pet.
subject: ELA

question: Leo is telling his class about his trip to the farm. Which sentences describe the farm with clear details AND express Leo’s feelings about the trip? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: The big pig rolled in the squishy mud and I felt excited to watch it.
answer_options.B: I felt scared when the loud rooster crowed right next to me.

answer_explanation: A is correct because it gives a specific detail about the pig and clearly expresses the feeling of excitement. B is correct because it describes a specific event (the rooster crowing) and clearly states the feeling of being scared. C is incorrect because it is too vague and does not express any feeling. D is incorrect because it states a general preference without describing specific details about the farm trip or expressing a clear feeling about that day.
subject: ELA

question: Tom is telling his class about the snow day he had yesterday. Which sentences help describe the snow day or tell how Tom felt? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: The snow was white and soft like cotton.
answer_options.B: I felt happy when I made a big snowman.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because it uses relevant sensory details ('white and soft like cotton') to describe the snow from the snow day. Option B is correct because it clearly expresses a feeling ('happy') and includes a relevant detail about the event ('made a big snowman'). Option C is incorrect because stating a favorite color does not describe the snow day or express feelings about it. Option D is incorrect because mentioning a new teacher is unrelated to describing the snow day.
subject: ELA

question: Lily is telling her friends about her birthday party. She says: 'I felt surprised when everyone jumped out and shouted Happy Birthday! Then we ate chocolate cake with candles. I was so happy that my friends came.' Which visual displays can Lily add to her description to clarify her ideas, thoughts, and feelings? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: A drawing of friends jumping out and shouting
answer_options.B: A drawing of a chocolate cake with candles

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because it shows the specific moment Lily described (friends jumping out and shouting), which clarifies why she felt surprised. Option B is correct because it shows the exact cake mentioned (chocolate with candles), clarifying that specific idea. Option C is incorrect because Lily stated she felt happy and surprised, not sad. Option D is incorrect because Lily specifically described eating chocolate cake, not vanilla cupcakes.
subject: ELA

question: Leo is telling the class about his birthday party. He says, 'My cake was very tall with three layers and pink frosting. I felt so happy that my smile was huge!' Which visual displays can Leo add to help his classmates understand what he is describing? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: A drawing of the tall cake with three layers and pink frosting
answer_options.B: A list of the guests who came to the party
answer_options.C: A photo of Leo smiling big at his party
answer_options.D: A chart showing how many birthdays are in each month

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because a drawing of the cake shows exactly what Leo described, helping classmates see the tall layers and pink frosting. Option C is correct because a photo of Leo smiling shows his happy feeling, which clarifies how he felt at the party. Option B is incorrect because a list of names is not a visual display that shows what the cake looked like or how Leo felt. Option D is incorrect because a chart about birthday months does not help clarify the description of Leo's specific cake or his happiness at that party.
subject: ELA

question: Your teacher asks you to tell what you did after school yesterday. Which sentences are complete and answer your teacher's question? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: I played with my dog in the yard.
answer_options.B: Went to the store with my mom.
answer_options.C: I will go to the park tomorrow.
answer_options.D: After school I drew a picture.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because it is a complete sentence with a subject (I) and a verb (played), and it appropriately tells about a past activity that happened after school yesterday. Option D is correct because it is a complete sentence with a subject (I) and a verb (drew), and it appropriately describes a completed action from after school yesterday. Option B is incorrect because it is a sentence fragment; it is missing the subject 'I' and does not express a complete thought. Option C is incorrect because, although it is grammatically a complete sentence with a subject and verb, it is not appropriate to the situation; it describes a future plan (tomorrow) rather than what happened yesterday after school.
subject: ELA

question: Your teacher asks, 'What did you do at recess today?' Which are complete sentences that answer her question? Select ALL that apply.
answer_explanation: Option A is correct because it is a complete sentence with a naming part (I) and an action part (played) that appropriately tells what happened at recess. Option D is correct because it is a complete sentence with a naming part (I) and an action part (ran) that answers the teacher's question about recess. Option B is incorrect because, although it is a complete sentence, it does not fit the situation; it tells about liking food instead of what happened at recess. Option C is incorrect because it is missing the naming part (who played?) and therefore is not a complete sentence.
subject: ELA

question:
Read this draft of an opinion piece written by a first grader:

'Dogs are the best pets. They can play catch with you. They give good hugs.'

The writer needs to add one more sentence to give the piece a sense of closure. Which of the following sentences could the writer use? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: That is why I love dogs.
answer_options.D: Dogs are great pets to have.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because 'That is why I love dogs' provides closure by referring back to the opinion (dogs are the best pets) and wrapping up the piece. Option D is correct because it restates the opinion in a concluding way, giving the piece a sense of ending. Option B is incorrect because it adds a new detail about the writer's specific dog rather than closing the piece about dogs in general. Option C is incorrect because it asks the reader a question instead of providing a closing statement about the writer's opinion.
subject: ELA

question:
Read this short opinion piece draft. Which sentences include the parts needed for a good opinion piece? Select ALL that apply.

Draft:
"I love The Very Hungry Caterpillar. It is the best book ever. The pictures are pretty. The caterpillar eats a lot of food."

answer_options.D: "The caterpillar eats a lot of food."

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because it names the book the writer is writing about, which introduces the topic. Option B is correct because it clearly states the writer's opinion that the book is the best. Option C is correct because it supplies a reason (the pretty pictures) to support why the book is good. Option D is incorrect because it simply tells a detail from the story; it does not introduce the topic, state an opinion, give a reason for the opinion, or provide closure.
subject: ELA

question: Read this report: 'This report is about elephants. Elephants are very large animals. They use their long trunks to spray water. That is all I know about elephants.' Which sentences give FACTS about elephants? Select all that apply.

answer_options.C: They use their long trunks to spray water.
answer_options.D: That is all I know about elephants.

answer_explanation: Option B and Option C are correct because they provide specific facts and information about elephants—their size and how they use their trunks. Option A is incorrect because it names the topic of the report but does not give a fact about elephants. Option D is incorrect because it is a closing sentence that signals the end of the report rather than providing information about the topic.
subject: ELA

question: A student is writing an informative report about butterflies. Which sentences tell facts that should be included in the report? Select all that apply.

answer_options.A: Butterflies begin life as eggs and hatch into caterpillars.
answer_options.B: Butterflies are the most beautiful insects in the world.
answer_options.C: Caterpillars form a chrysalis before becoming butterflies.
answer_options.D: I saw a butterfly in my garden yesterday.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because it provides a true fact about how butterflies begin their lives. Option C is correct because it provides a true fact about how caterpillars change into butterflies. Option B is incorrect because 'most beautiful' is an opinion, not a fact suitable for an informative report. Option D is incorrect because it tells a personal story rather than a fact about butterflies.
subject: ELA

question: Maya wrote the beginning of her story: 'Yesterday I went to the beach. First, I put on my sunscreen.' She wants to add more sentences that show what happened next in order and give her story a good ending. Which sentences could Maya write? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Next I built a big sandcastle. Last I watched the waves wash it away.
answer_options.B: Then I swam in the ocean. Finally it was time to go home.
answer_options.C: I like the beach because it is fun.
answer_options.D: After that I found a shell. Then I looked for more crabs.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because it uses the time words 'Next' and 'Last' to show the order of two events, and the word 'Last' signals the ending of the story. Option B is correct because it uses 'Then' to show the next event and 'Finally' to show the story is closing. Option C is incorrect because it gives an opinion without telling what happened next or using time words to show order, and it does not have an ending sentence. Option D is incorrect because although it uses time words 'After that' and 'Then' to show order, it does not give a sense of closure or an ending; looking for more crabs suggests the story continues.
subject: ELA

question: Tom is writing a story about his morning. Read these sentences from his story. Which sentences show events in order using time words like 'first' or 'then,' OR a closing sentence that wraps up the story? Select ALL that apply.
answer_explanation: A is correct because it uses the time word 'First' to signal the beginning of the event sequence. C is correct because it uses the time word 'Then' to show the next event in order. D is correct because it uses 'Finally' to signal the end and tells how the narrator feels, giving the story a sense of closure. B is incorrect because it only provides a descriptive detail and does not show event order or provide an ending.
subject: ELA

question: Max wrote: 'I visited the beach.' His friend asked, 'What did you see at the beach?' Max wants to make his story stronger by answering his friend’s question. Select ALL the details Max could add that tell what he saw at the beach.

answer_options.A: I saw a red crab in the sand.
answer_options.B: The beach is close to my home.
answer_options.C: I like to swim in the pool.
answer_options.D: We found a big white seashell.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because 'I saw a red crab in the sand' answers the friend’s question by adding a specific detail about what Max saw at the beach. Option D is correct because 'We found a big white seashell' also answers the question by describing another thing Max saw. Option B is incorrect because it tells where the beach is located, not what Max saw there. Option C is incorrect because it talks about a swimming pool, which is not related to the beach topic.
subject: ELA

question: Tara writes: "We had fun at the beach." Her classmate asks, "What did you do there?" Which sentences could Tara add to answer her classmate's question and strengthen her writing? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: I built a big sandcastle with a moat around it.
answer_options.B: We collected smooth shells in a red bucket.
answer_options.C: My brother likes to play video games at home.
answer_options.D: I have a pet fish named Bubbles.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because it answers what Tara did at the beach (built a sandcastle), which adds a specific detail that responds to the classmate's question and strengthens the writing. Option B is correct because it tells what they did (collected shells), providing relevant detail about the beach trip that answers the question. Option C is incorrect because it talks about the brother's hobbies at home, which is off-topic and does not answer what Tara did at the beach. Option D is incorrect because it introduces a pet fish, which is unrelated to the beach topic and does not answer the classmate's question about activities at the beach.
subject: ELA

question: Ms. Lee's first graders are using digital tools with her guidance to work on writing. Which students are using digital tools to produce or publish their writing? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Tom and his partner use a tablet to type a story about dogs, then email it to their teacher.
answer_options.B: Sara draws a picture with crayons and writes her name on the paper.
answer_options.C: Jake and Kim use a computer to write a poem, then print it to hang in the hallway.
answer_options.D: Leo uses a video game to play a racing game with his friend.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because Tom uses a tablet (a digital tool) with his partner (collaboration) to type a story (producing writing) and emails it to the teacher (publishing), all with Ms. Lee's guidance. Option C is correct because Jake and Kim use a computer (a digital tool) together (collaboration) to write a poem (producing writing) and print it to display (publishing), all with Ms. Lee's guidance. Option B is incorrect because crayons are physical tools, not digital tools. Option D is incorrect because playing a racing video game is not an activity that produces or publishes writing, even though it uses a digital tool and involves a friend.
subject: ELA

question: Ms. Lee’s first graders are working on a class book. Which of these show the students using digital tools to produce or publish their writing? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: The students type their stories on classroom tablets.
answer_options.B: The teacher helps students post stories on a class website.
answer_options.C: The students draw pictures with colored pencils.
answer_options.D: The students use a computer to print copies to share with classmates.

answer_explanation: Options A, B, and D are correct because they describe using digital tools to produce or publish writing. In option A, tablets are digital tools and typing is producing writing. In option B, a class website is a digital publishing tool, and the teacher’s help satisfies the 'guidance and support from adults' requirement. In option D, a computer and printer are digital tools used to publish and share writing with peers. Option C is incorrect because colored pencils are physical art supplies, not digital tools.
subject: ELA

question: A first-grade class is doing a shared project about how to plant a seed. They explored two 'how-to' books to learn what to do. Which choices show the class is following the project rules correctly? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Write the steps in the order they happen
answer_options.B: Work with friends to write the instructions
answer_options.C: Look at the books to learn what to do
answer_options.D: Write the steps in any order they want

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because writing steps in order creates a sequence of instructions, which is required for a how-to book. Option B is correct because working with friends shows participation in a shared writing project. Option C is correct because looking at the books shows they are using sources to research the topic. Option D is incorrect because writing steps in random order would make the instructions confusing and does not create a proper sequence.
subject: ELA

question: Your class is writing a 'how-to' book about making a peanut butter sandwich. Which sentences are good steps to include? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: First, get two slices of bread.
answer_options.B: Next, spread peanut butter on one slice.
answer_options.C: Peanut butter is brown and creamy.
answer_options.D: Write the steps in any order.

answer_explanation: Options A and B are correct because they give clear instructions that tell the reader exactly what to do and use sequence words ('First' and 'Next') to show the order of steps, which is how you write a sequence of instructions for a 'how-to' book. Option C is incorrect because it only describes what peanut butter looks like rather than telling someone how to make the sandwich. Option D is incorrect because when writing instructions, the steps must be in the correct order so the reader can follow them; putting them in 'any order' would confuse the reader and is not how 'how-to' books are written.
subject: ELA

question: Read the passage. "Elephants are large animals. They use their long trunks to pick up food. Elephants eat plants and fruit. They drink water with their trunks. Elephants take baths in rivers." Tina is reading to learn how elephants use their trunks. Which facts from the passage answer Tina's question? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.B: They use their long trunks to pick up food.
answer_options.D: They drink water with their trunks.

answer_explanation: Option B is correct because the passage states that elephants use their long trunks to pick up food. Option D is correct because the passage states that elephants drink water with their trunks. Option A is incorrect because it only describes the size of elephants, not how they use their trunks. Option C is incorrect because it tells what elephants eat, but does not mention that they use their trunks to eat.
subject: ELA

question: Read the sentences. Max asked, 'What pets does my friend Anna have?' Anna wrote: 'I have a soft cat and a loud dog. I feed them every day.' Which sentences give information that answers Max's question? Select ALL that apply.
answer_explanation: Option A is correct because it tells exactly one pet Anna has, which answers Max's question about what pets she owns. Option B is correct because it names the second pet Anna has. Option C is incorrect because it describes how Anna cares for the pets rather than identifying what the pets are. Option D is incorrect because the text does not mention a fish; it only mentions a cat and a dog.
subject: ELA

question: The uppercase letter is 'T'. Type the matching lowercase letter: ______
answer_explanation: This question assesses the student's ability to print the lowercase form of a given uppercase letter, which is part of the skill to print all upper- and lowercase letters. The uppercase letter 'T' corresponds to the lowercase letter 't'.
subject: ELA

question: Look at these three letters: uppercase B, uppercase D, and uppercase P. Write the lowercase letter that matches the uppercase B: ______
answer_explanation: The uppercase letter 'B' corresponds to the lowercase letter 'b'.
